project.name: Msanii
project.description: Msanii is a music industry management platform for artists, managers, and collaborators, handling artist profiles, project and work management, rights registration, contract analysis, royalty calculations, and collaboration workflows. Note: this project has over 100 source files; consider scoping analysis to a subdirectory for faster results.

nodes.name: App.tsx
nodes.summary: Root application component that sets up React Router routes, wraps the app in providers, and lazy-loads all page components with a Suspense loading fallback.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses React.lazy + Suspense for route-level code splitting, plus small inline helper components (PageLoader, PageTimer, AdminPosthogTagger) for cross-cutting concerns.

nodes.name: App

nodes.name: useAdmin.ts
nodes.summary: React Query hooks for admin functionality — checking current-user admin status, listing/searching users, mutating user admin/plan/storage state, and fetching pro-upgrade requests.

nodes.name: useIsAdmin
nodes.summary: Hook that checks whether the current user is an admin via a cached analytics-context short-circuit plus a backend query, with a client-side event to force re-checks.

nodes.name: useAdminUsers
nodes.summary: React Query hook that fetches a paginated, searchable list of users for the admin users table.

nodes.name: useAdminMutations
nodes.summary: Bundle of mutation hooks (grant/revoke pro, apply/clear entitlement overrides, promote/demote admin, recalc storage) that invalidate related admin queries on success.

nodes.name: useProRequests
nodes.summary: React Query hook that fetches pending/approved/rejected pro-upgrade requests for the admin dashboard.

nodes.name: useAdminAnalyticsSummary.ts
nodes.summary: React Query hook that fetches the admin tool-usage analytics summary (funnel counts, per-tool stats) for a given time window and cohort.

nodes.name: useAdminAnalyticsSummary
nodes.summary: Fetches the admin analytics summary endpoint via apiFetch for the requested window/cohort and caches the result with React Query.

nodes.name: useAdminBehaviorSummary.ts
nodes.summary: React Query hook that fetches site-wide behavior analytics (pageviews, top pages, top flows) for the admin behavior dashboard card.

nodes.name: useAdminBehaviorSummary
nodes.summary: Resolves the current user/cohort context and fetches behavior analytics data via apiFetch, cached with React Query.

nodes.name: useAdminPosthogTag.ts
nodes.summary: Hook that tags the current PostHog-identified user with their admin status so analytics dashboards can segment by admin/non-admin.

nodes.name: useAdminPosthogTag
nodes.summary: Effect hook that re-identifies the user in PostHog with an is_admin property whenever auth or admin status changes.

nodes.name: useAdminStatus.ts
nodes.summary: Lightweight external-store hook exposing the cached admin flag, updated reactively when the analytics-context cache changes.

nodes.name: useAdminStatus
nodes.summary: Uses useSyncExternalStore over a window-event-driven cache to return the current user's cached admin flag without a network round trip.

nodes.name: useAnalyticsContext.ts
nodes.summary: Core hook and localStorage-backed cache for the user's analytics context (plan, role, admin/tester flags) — fetched once from the backend, cached, and re-identified into PostHog; also exposes helpers to peek/refresh the cache from anywhere in the app.

nodes.name: readCache
nodes.summary: Reads and parses the cached analytics context for a user from localStorage.

nodes.name: peekCachedAnalyticsContext
nodes.summary: Public helper that synchronously peeks the cached analytics context without triggering a fetch, used by other hooks to short-circuit.

nodes.name: writeCache
nodes.summary: Writes an analytics-context cache entry to localStorage and dispatches an update event for cross-hook subscribers.

nodes.name: clearCache
nodes.summary: Clears the analytics-context cache and notifies subscribers, used on sign-out.

nodes.name: refreshAnalyticsContext
nodes.summary: Public helper to force-clear and refetch the analytics context for a user, used after actions that change plan/role.

nodes.name: fetchAndIdentify
nodes.summary: Fetches the analytics context from the backend, writes it to cache, and identifies the user in PostHog with the resulting properties.

nodes.name: useAnalyticsContext
nodes.summary: Main hook that loads the analytics context on mount/auth-change, keeps it fresh on window focus, and identifies the user in PostHog.

nodes.name: useArtistsList.ts
nodes.summary: React Query hook that fetches the current user's list of artist profiles for portfolio/subscription pages.

nodes.name: useArtistsList

nodes.name: usePageTimer.ts
nodes.summary: Hook that measures time spent on each route and reports it to PostHog on navigation/visibility change/unmount.

nodes.name: usePageTimer
nodes.summary: Tracks elapsed time on the current page using route location changes and page-visibility/unload events, flushing a duration event via PostHog's capture().

nodes.name: useTesterGrants.ts
nodes.summary: React Query hooks to list, create, and revoke tester program grants from the admin panel.

nodes.name: useTesterGrants

nodes.name: useCreateTesterGrant
nodes.summary: Mutation hook that creates a new tester grant for a user by email and invalidates the grants list on success.

nodes.name: useRevokeTesterGrant
nodes.summary: Mutation hook that revokes an existing tester grant and invalidates the grants list on success.

nodes.name: useTesterStatus.ts
nodes.summary: External-store hook exposing the cached tester-grant status (active/expiry) reactively from the analytics-context cache.

nodes.name: useTesterStatus
nodes.summary: Uses useSyncExternalStore to return the current user's cached tester status without a network round trip, re-rendering on cache updates.

nodes.name: posthog.ts
nodes.summary: PostHog client wrapper providing init, identify, reset, and capture functions used throughout the frontend for product analytics.

nodes.name: initPostHog

nodes.summary: Identifies the current user in PostHog with a set of properties (plan, role, admin/tester flags).

nodes.name: resetUser
nodes.summary: Resets the PostHog client's identity, used on sign-out.

nodes.name: capture
nodes.summary: Thin wrapper around posthog.capture for firing a named analytics event with properties.

nodes.name: events.py

nodes.name: on
nodes.summary: Registers a handler function to be invoked when the named event is emitted.

nodes.name: emit
nodes.summary: Asynchronously invokes all handlers registered for an event name with the given payload, isolating failures per handler.

nodes.name: _safe_call
nodes.summary: Invokes a single event handler (sync or async) and swallows/logs exceptions so one failing handler doesn't break emit for the others.

nodes.name: encrypt_token

nodes.name: verify_oauth_state

nodes.name: refresh_access_token

nodes.name: get_valid_token

nodes.name: store_connection

The single biggest and most central file in the backend.
nodes.languageNotes: At 3095 lines, this file mounts ~20 routers via app.include_router() and additionally implements several of the app's largest endpoints (a 429-line streaming royalty calculator, a 162-line Zoe chat stream) directly rather than delegating to a router module.

nodes.name: _coerce_iso_date
nodes.summary: Best-effort coercion of a raw date value from statements or contracts into an ISO date string, tolerating multiple input formats.

nodes.name: CalculationError
nodes.summary: Custom exception raised when royalty calculation fails, carrying structured context about the failure for API error responses.

nodes.name: RoyaltyPayment
nodes.summary: Dataclass representing a single computed royalty payment line — song, party, role, royalty type, percentage, gross/net amounts, and expenses applied.

nodes.name: CalcOutput

nodes.name: StatementRow
nodes.summary: Dataclass representing a single normalized row read from a royalty statement (CSV/Excel), covering sales, units, distribution vendor, and currency fields.

nodes.name: RoyaltyCalculator
nodes.summary: The central OneClick engine: reads royalty statements, merges multiple contracts, runs basis auditing and split verification, computes per-party payments, and exports results to Excel/JSON.
nodes.languageNotes: 1250-line class with 17 methods spanning statement I/O, contract merging, and payment calculation.

nodes.name: _bucket_for
nodes.summary: Classifies a royalty type string into the master or publishing split bucket (or drops it) using the shared type-bucket sets.

nodes.name: parse_royalty_splits
nodes.summary: Parses a contract PDF via MusicContractParser and pivots its royalty shares into per-party master/publishing percentage splits for the Registry Add Work wizard, flagging whether the main artist was found.

nodes.name: audit_contract_basis
nodes.summary: Asks an LLM to detect an explicit income-basis clause (net receipts, deduction, wholesale/retail) in contract markdown, requiring the returned clause to be verbatim-present before accepting a finding.

nodes.name: log_basis_finding
nodes.summary: Logs a detected basis finding to the audit logger and fires a PostHog analytics event for human review, without changing any calculated payout.

nodes.name: BasisFinding
nodes.summary: Dataclass capturing a detected income-basis clause: the basis type, implied factor, verbatim quote, affected royalty types, and optional reference-book context.

nodes.name: effective_basis
nodes.summary: Resolves the effective income basis (net/gross) for a royalty share, falling back to the contract's default basis when the share doesn't specify one.

nodes.name: Party
nodes.summary: Dataclass representing a contract party by name and role (artist, label, songwriter, etc.).

nodes.name: Work
nodes.summary: Dataclass representing a musical work referenced in a contract, with its title and work type.

nodes.name: RoyaltyShare
nodes.summary: Dataclass representing one party's royalty percentage for a given royalty type, including terms and income basis.

nodes.name: ContractData
nodes.summary: Top-level dataclass aggregating all parties, works, and royalty shares extracted from a single contract, plus a summary and default basis.

nodes.name: MusicContractParser
nodes.summary: LLM-driven parser that extracts a full ContractData structure (parties, works, royalty shares) from contract markdown in one unified call, normalizing names, titles, and roles as it builds each record.

nodes.name: _pct_in_quote
nodes.summary: Checks whether a percentage value's digits literally appear within a quoted contract clause, used as a guardrail against false-positive verifications.

nodes.name: _match_reported
nodes.summary: Matches an LLM-reported royalty share (blind re-read of the contract) against the corresponding extracted share by party name and royalty type.

nodes.name: _judge
nodes.summary: Computes the per-share verdict (match/mismatch/unverified) by comparing an extracted royalty share's percentage and basis against the LLM's blind reported value, requiring verbatim clause support.

nodes.name: verify_royalty_shares
nodes.summary: Orchestrates the advisory split-verification pass: prompts an LLM to blindly report each party's contract percentages, judges each extracted share against the response, and returns an overall ReviewResult.

nodes.name: SplitFinding
nodes.summary: Dataclass capturing the verification outcome for a single royalty share: extracted vs. contract percentage/basis, verdict, supporting quote, and note.

nodes.name: ReviewResult
nodes.summary: Dataclass aggregating the overall verification verdict plus the list of per-share SplitFinding results for a contract.

nodes.name: embeddings.py
nodes.summary: OpenAI embedding helpers for the reference-book ingestion pipeline, including deterministic vector ID generation for idempotent upserts into Pinecone.

nodes.name: pdf_markdown.py

nodes.name: sections.py
nodes.summary: Detects and categorizes markdown sections in contract text (royalty calculations, publishing rights, termination, etc.) using heading heuristics and keyword classification, feeding the ingestion pipeline's chunking.

nodes.name: tables.py
nodes.summary: Detects, extracts, and linearizes markdown tables from contract documents into both preserved raw text (for LLM extraction) and natural-language sentences (for embedding similarity).

nodes.name: client.py
nodes.summary: Shared, lazily-initialized OpenAI client singleton used across all backend contract-parsing and chatbot modules.

nodes.name: normalize.py
nodes.summary: Shared text-normalization helpers for fuzzy-matching song titles, party names, and collaborator roles across OneClick, Zoe, and the Registry.

nodes.name: contract_chatbot.py
nodes.summary: Core RAG-based chatbot engine powering Zoe: query routing/classification, section-aware retrieval with similarity thresholds, pinned-fact and assumption ledgers, conversation memory/summarization, and streaming LLM answer generation grounded in contract context.
nodes.languageNotes: The ContractChatbot class (~3500 lines, 65 methods) is a very large single class handling routing, retrieval, memory, and streaming generation — a strong candidate for decomposition.

nodes.name: create_embeddings
nodes.summary: Generates OpenAI embeddings for a batch of text chunks using the configured embedding model, for upsert into the Pinecone reference index.

nodes.name: create_query_embedding
nodes.summary: Generates a single OpenAI embedding for a search query, used by search_reference to perform vector similarity search.

nodes.name: generate_deterministic_id
nodes.summary: Derives a stable, content-hash-based vector ID from a chunk's text and metadata so re-ingestion upserts idempotently instead of duplicating vectors.

nodes.name: pdf_to_markdown
nodes.summary: Converts a PDF file to markdown text using pymupdf4llm, embedding [[PAGE n]] navigation markers for later page-citation support.

nodes.name: markdown_has_page_markers
nodes.summary: Checks whether markdown text still contains raw [[PAGE n]] navigation markers.

nodes.name: strip_page_markers
nodes.summary: Removes [[PAGE n]] navigation markers (and surrounding blank lines) from markdown so they never leak into user-facing prose or OneClick's contract text.

nodes.name: split_into_sections
nodes.summary: Splits contract markdown into discrete sections using heading detection, producing chunks aligned to the document's own structure for downstream embedding and retrieval.

nodes.name: is_semantic_heading
nodes.summary: Heuristically determines whether a line of text is a genuine section heading (vs. body text) based on formatting and length cues.

nodes.name: categorize_section
nodes.summary: Classifies a section heading into a predefined category (royalty calculations, publishing rights, termination, etc.) via keyword matching against SECTION_CATEGORIES.

nodes.name: detect_and_extract_tables
nodes.summary: Scans markdown text for table blocks (header + separator + data rows), extracting each as an atomic TableBlock with its preceding context preserved.

nodes.name: linearize_table
nodes.summary: Converts a raw markdown table into natural-language sentences so its content embeds with good semantic similarity for retrieval.

nodes.name: categorize_table_content
nodes.summary: Classifies a table's content category using its text and preceding context against the shared SECTION_CATEGORIES keyword sets.

nodes.name: split_table_if_oversized
nodes.summary: Splits an oversized table block into smaller chunks bounded by a max character count, preserving header context in each split.

nodes.name: TableBlock
nodes.summary: Dataclass representing one detected markdown table with its raw text, preceding context, and source line range.

nodes.name: get_openai_client
nodes.summary: Lazily initializes and returns a shared OpenAI client singleton, reading OPENAI_API_KEY/OPENAI_BASE_URL from the environment and raising if the key is missing.

nodes.name: normalize_title
nodes.summary: Normalizes a song/work title for fuzzy matching against royalty statement rows, stripping punctuation and casing differences.

nodes.name: titles_match
nodes.summary: Fuzzy-compares two normalized titles to determine whether they refer to the same song, tolerating minor wording differences.

nodes.name: find_matching_song
nodes.summary: Fuzzy-matches a contract song title against a statement's song totals dictionary and returns the aggregated matched amounts.

nodes.name: simplify_role
nodes.summary: Collapses verbose contract role strings (e.g. 'lyrical writer (songwriter)') into a small canonical role taxonomy (writer, producer, label, etc.).

nodes.name: normalize_name
nodes.summary: Normalizes a party or artist name for case-insensitive comparison across contracts and statements.

nodes.name: parse_page_citations
nodes.summary: Parses raw page-citation markers out of an LLM answer so Zoe can attribute claims to specific contract pages.

nodes.name: build_labeled_context
nodes.summary: Builds a labeled, multi-contract context string (prefixing each contract's markdown with its name) for prompts spanning several contracts.

nodes.name: build_selection_note
nodes.summary: Builds a human-readable note describing which of several candidate contracts were actually selected/processed for a query, for transparency in Zoe's answer.

nodes.name: ConfidenceLevel
nodes.summary: Enum of graduated confidence tiers (high/medium/low) used to decide how directly Zoe answers a query and whether to add a caveat.

nodes.name: RouteDecision
nodes.summary: Dataclass capturing the outcome of query routing: which route to take, the answer mode, confidence, and reasoning.

nodes.name: Scope
nodes.summary: Represents the current conversation scope (artist/project/contract), with helpers to test scope match, detect a global scope, and serialize for logging.

nodes.name: PinnedFact
nodes.summary: Represents a fact extracted from a contract and pinned to the conversation with a confidence score, source reference, and scope, so it can be reused across turns without re-retrieval.

nodes.name: Assumption
nodes.summary: Represents an assumption Zoe introduced mid-conversation, trackable as verified/invalidated and tied to a scope so it can be invalidated when the scope changes.

nodes.name: FactLedger
nodes.summary: Per-session ledger storing pinned facts, with lookup by type and session-clearing support.

nodes.name: AssumptionLedger
nodes.summary: Per-session ledger tracking assumptions Zoe has made, including scope-based invalidation and verification of individual assumptions.

nodes.name: ConversationSummary
nodes.summary: Maintains a rolling summary of the conversation so older turns can be compressed out of the LLM context window while preserving key facts.

nodes.name: ChatMessage
nodes.summary: Dataclass representing a single chat turn with role, content, timestamp, and optional metadata.

nodes.name: InMemoryChatMessageHistory
nodes.summary: In-memory per-session chat history store handling message storage, LLM-formatted retrieval, token-count estimation, trimming to fit context windows, and tracking of the last-discussed contract IDs and pending suggestions.

nodes.name: ContractChatbot

nodes.languageNotes: A ~3500-line, 65-method class covering query classification, retrieval, memory management, and streaming generation — the largest single class in the codebase.

nodes.name: chunking.py
nodes.summary: Pure, section-aware PDF chunking pipeline for RAG ingestion — extracts markdown pages via pymupdf4llm and splits them into token-bounded chunks whose section_path breadcrumbs and page ranges are computed from character offsets for accurate attribution at page/heading boundaries.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses dataclasses (PageText, Chunk) as lightweight, unit-testable data models, and keeps PDF extraction (extract_pages) deliberately separate from the pure chunking logic (section_aware_chunks) to ease testing.

nodes.name: PageText
nodes.summary: Dataclass representing one extracted PDF page as markdown, with its 1-based page number.

nodes.name: Chunk
nodes.summary: Dataclass representing a single text chunk ready for embedding, carrying its section path breadcrumb, page range, chunk index, and token count.

nodes.name: extract_pages
nodes.summary: Extracts each page of a PDF as markdown text via pymupdf4llm and returns a list of PageText records.

nodes.name: _build_combined
nodes.summary: Concatenates extracted pages into a single markdown string while recording the character-offset boundary of each page, enabling later offset-to-page lookups.

nodes.name: _sections_with_offsets
nodes.summary: Scans the combined markdown for heading lines and builds a list of (character offset, section_path breadcrumb) pairs by tracking a heading-level stack.

nodes.name: section_aware_chunks
nodes.summary: Public entry point that splits extracted pages into token-bounded Chunk objects using a tiktoken-based recursive splitter, attaching the correct section_path breadcrumb and page range to each chunk via offset lookups.

nodes.name: ingest.py
nodes.summary: Orchestrates idempotent Pinecone ingestion of chunked reference-book text: pre-flight index/dimension validation, deterministic vector IDs for safe re-ingestion, retry-wrapped OpenAI embedding and Pinecone upsert/delete calls.
nodes.languageNotes: Pinecone and OpenAI clients are injected as parameters rather than constructed internally, keeping the module unit-testable without live network calls; only a defined set of transient exception names/HTTP statuses are retried.

nodes.name: slugify
nodes.summary: Lowercases and hyphenates a string into an ID/prefix-safe slug, raising ValueError if the result would be empty.

nodes.name: preflight
nodes.summary: Validates a Pinecone index before ingestion — checks embedding dimension matches, page-count/extraction-quality floor, and other sanity conditions, raising ValueError on failure.

nodes.name: build_vector
nodes.summary: Assembles a Pinecone upsert record (id, embedding values, metadata) from a Chunk, its source slug, book title, and a precomputed embedding vector.

nodes.name: delete_existing_source
nodes.summary: Deletes all previously-upserted vectors for a given source slug within a namespace, used to support idempotent re-ingestion (--replace mode).

nodes.name: ingest_chunks
nodes.summary: Main ingestion loop: batches chunks, requests OpenAI embeddings, builds Pinecone vectors, and upserts them in batches with retry wrapping, returning the total vector count.

nodes.name: pinecone_upload_pdf.py
nodes.summary: Standalone CLI script that chunks a reference PDF (via knowledge.chunking) and embeds/upserts it into a Pinecone namespace (via knowledge.ingest), printing section-path coverage diagnostics and supporting --replace and --dry-run modes.
nodes.languageNotes: Manipulates sys.path at import time so the sibling `knowledge` package is importable when the script is invoked directly (python rag-tools/pinecone_upload_pdf.py ...) rather than as an installed module.

nodes.name: _print_section_diagnostic
nodes.summary: Prints a diagnostic report on what percentage of produced chunks have a non-empty section_path, warning when the source PDF appears to lack markdown headers so 'section-aware' chunking degrades to plain token windows.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: CLI entry point: parses arguments, extracts and chunks the given PDF, validates/connects to the Pinecone index, optionally deletes existing vectors for the source, then embeds and upserts all chunks via OpenAI + Pinecone.

nodes.name: Dockerfile
nodes.summary: Single-stage Docker build for the Msanii FastAPI backend: starts from python:3.11-slim, installs gcc/g++ build tools, installs Python dependencies via Poetry (system-wide, no venv), copies the application source, and launches the server with Uvicorn on port 8080 to match Cloud Run's expected port.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses `poetry config virtualenvs.create false` so dependencies install directly into the container's system Python, avoiding a redundant virtualenv layer in the image; CMD uses shell form so `${PORT}` is expanded at container start, letting Cloud Run override the port via env var.

nodes.name: .dockerignore
nodes.summary: Docker build-context exclusion list for the backend image — excludes Python bytecode caches, virtualenvs, IDE settings, test/coverage artifacts, markdown docs, sample files, logs, and VCS metadata to keep the build context small and avoid leaking local dev files into the image.

nodes.name: ci.yml

nodes.name: deploy-backend-dev.yml

nodes.languageNotes: Uses GCP Workload Identity Federation (google-github-actions/auth) instead of long-lived service-account key secrets, and separates non-secret --update-env-vars from GSM-backed --update-secrets on the Cloud Run deploy.

nodes.name: deploy-backend.yml
nodes.summary: GitHub Actions workflow that builds and deploys the production backend Docker image to Cloud Run (msanii-backend) on `v*` tag pushes; authenticates via GCP Workload Identity Federation, verifies the deployed /health endpoint, and conditionally clears the OneClick cache by diffing changed files against the previous release tag.
nodes.languageNotes: Determines whether to wipe the OneClick calc cache by diffing src/backend/oneclick/** against the previous v* git tag (excluding oneclick/royalties/**, which is persistence-only), rather than relying on a path filter on the push event.

nodes.name: deploy-frontend-prod.yml

tour.title: What Is Msanii?
tour.description: Start here to understand the product before touching code. Msanii is a music-industry management platform where artists, managers, and collaborators run their day-to-day work: artist profiles, projects/works, rights registration, contract analysis, royalty math, and collaboration. The README covers the stack (React + TypeScript frontend, FastAPI/Python backend, Supabase/Postgres with RLS) and setup, while the docs index maps each product surface you will tour next, and package.json lists the exact scripts and dependencies.

tour.title: Frontend Boot & Routing
tour.description: Every user session begins here. main.tsx initializes PostHog analytics and mounts the root App into the DOM; App.tsx sets up React Router, wraps everything in the auth and query providers, and lazy-loads each page so the initial bundle stays small. This is the map of every route a user can reach, and where global styles from index.css are applied.
tour.languageLesson: React.lazy() + <Suspense> code-splits each page into its own chunk that loads on demand, so the app ships a tiny initial bundle and fetches page code only when the user navigates there.

tour.title: Sign-In & Access Control Flow
tour.description: Follow a user logging in. The Auth page collects credentials and calls into AuthContext, the single app-wide provider that holds Supabase session state and exposes sign-in/sign-up/OAuth actions (plus PostHog identify and welcome-email side effects). ProtectedRoute reads that context to guard authenticated pages, and on the backend auth.py verifies the Supabase JWT on nearly every request. This dual-layer model — project members vs. work-only collaborators — is enforced by Postgres RLS downstream.
tour.languageLesson: The backend validates Supabase-issued JWTs against the project's JWKS (public keys) rather than a shared secret, exposing FastAPI dependency functions that inject the verified user id/email into route handlers.

tour.title: The Backend Spine & FE↔BE Bridge
tour.description: This is how the two halves of the app talk. On the frontend, apiFetch.ts wraps every backend call, attaching the Supabase auth header, normalizing JSON, and throwing a typed ApiError on failure. On the backend, main.py is the most central file: it mounts every router, defines the verify_user_owns_* authorization helpers, and even implements the biggest endpoints (upload, Zoe chat stream, OneClick calculation) directly. pagination.py shows the shared response conventions.
tour.languageLesson: Because the backend uses a service-role Supabase client that bypasses RLS, every endpoint takes a user_id and performs its own ownership check via verify_user_owns_* helpers — per-endpoint authorization is the real access-control layer, not the database policies alone.

tour.title: Projects & Portfolio Flow
tour.description: The core content surface users live in. Portfolio.tsx shows projects grouped by artist; clicking one opens ProjectDetail.tsx, a tabbed view of works, files, audio, members, and settings. usePortfolioData is the React Query hook that fetches this data, calling the projects router.py, which delegates business logic (membership, sharing, content) to projects service.py. Everything downstream — registry, OneClick, Zoe — reads from this shared project/work data model.

tour.title: Registry Flow — Rights & Ownership
tour.description: Building on projects, this is how a work gets registered and shared. The Registry dashboard lists works; AddWorkWizard walks the user through creating one with master/publishing ownership stakes, then WorkDetail lets them invite collaborators with splits and roles. useRegistry (a top fan-in hook) drives all of it against registry router.py and service.py, which enforce the Draft → Pending → Registered lifecycle and the work-only collaborator access layer. This is the correctness-critical heart of ownership splits.

tour.title: OneClick Flow — Royalty Calculation
tour.description: The flagship tool, end-to-end. On OneClickDocuments.tsx a user uploads/selects a royalty statement and the matching contract, then runs the calculation; the results render in CalculationResults.tsx. The math lives in the backend royalty_calculator.py, which reads CSV/Excel statements, merges them with parsed contract terms, and computes per-party payments with expense allocation and split verification, while breakdown.py structures the earnings breakdown. The docs/oneclick.md guide explains the product intent behind the numbers.

tour.title: OneClick Flow — Getting People Paid
tour.description: Once royalties are calculated, this flow moves money. The Payments page and PaymentTracking component let users manage payees, periods, and payout runs; useRoyalties exposes the payees/periods/payout mutations including PayPal order creation and capture. The oneclick/royalties router.py and service.py implement the payout ledger and receipts on the backend. This closes the loop from 'what is owed' (Step 7) to 'who got paid.'

tour.title: Zoe Flow — AI Contract Analysis
tour.description: Msanii's AI assistant for reading contracts. The Zoe page hosts the chat; ZoeInputBar sends questions, useZoeData holds the selected artist/project/contract context, and useStreamingChat consumes the streamed response token-by-token. The backend engine contract_chatbot.py is a RAG pipeline: it classifies the query, retrieves section-aware contract context with similarity thresholds, keeps a pinned-fact ledger, and streams a grounded LLM answer. The actual stream endpoint lives in main.py from Step 4.
tour.languageLesson: Streaming works by having the FastAPI endpoint return a streamed response the browser reads incrementally via a ReadableStream, so tokens appear as the model generates them instead of waiting for the full answer — the RAG layer grounds each answer in retrieved contract chunks to reduce hallucination.

tour.title: Split Sheet Flow
tour.description: A focused document-generation tool. SplitSheet.tsx is a form that collects song title and contributor splits, then posts to the splitsheet router.py, which renders a formatted document via pdf_generator.py or docx_generator.py depending on the requested format. It is the simplest full FE→BE flow in the app, making it a clean model for how the other tools are structured. See docs/split-sheet.md for the product context.

tour.title: Workspace & Boards Flow
tour.description: How teams organize their work. The Workspace hub hosts Kanban boards; KanbanBoard.tsx renders draggable columns/cards and useBoards is the hook powering board and task state with optimistic updates. The backend boards router.py and service.py handle board/task CRUD, with team management layered alongside. This is the collaboration surface that ties artists, managers, and collaborators together day to day.

tour.title: Integrations Flow — Drive & Slack
tour.description: How Msanii connects to third-party services. The IntegrationHub surfaces connect buttons; useIntegrations lists a user's connections, and clicking Connect kicks off an OAuth handshake handled by the shared oauth.py (token encryption, refresh, and a signed state JWT). google_drive/router.py enables file browse/import/export, while slack/router.py wires channel notifications and @-mention webhooks. This is the plumbing behind 'share this PDF to Drive/Slack' actions elsewhere in the app.
tour.languageLesson: OAuth here uses a signed state JWT to defend the callback against CSRF and to carry which user/integration initiated the flow, and stores encrypted refresh tokens so the backend can silently mint new access tokens when they expire.

tour.title: Billing & Entitlements Flow
tour.description: How free vs. paid access is enforced. The Subscription page lets users manage their plan; useEntitlements exposes the current tier, feature flags, and usage caps, while useGatedAction wraps mutations to catch 402 responses and surface a paywall instead of a raw error. The backend billing_router.py starts Stripe checkout/portal sessions, and stripe_events.py processes webhooks to activate or cancel subscriptions. This gating wraps the tools you just toured (OneClick, Zoe, etc.).
tour.languageLesson: Feature gating leans on the HTTP 402 Payment Required status: gated endpoints return 402 when a plan cap is hit, and the frontend hook intercepts exactly that code to render an upgrade modal — Stripe webhooks are the source of truth for entitlement changes, not the client.

tour.title: Ship It — Build & Deployment
tour.description: The capstone: how all of the above reaches users. The backend Dockerfile packages the FastAPI app into a container image that Cloud Run runs on port 8080. The deploy-backend and deploy-frontend-prod GitHub Actions workflows push those images/builds on merges and tag releases, while ci.yml runs lint and tests as the quality gate, and vercel.json configures the frontend host. Together they define the path from a merged PR to production.
tour.languageLesson: GitHub Actions workflows trigger on `on:` events (push, tag, PR) and run `jobs` of sequential `steps`; here dev deploys fire on merge to main while prod deploys fire on a version tag, mirroring the app's two-environment release model.
